chapter_title.ar: باب الْمُتَوَفَّى عَنْهَا زَوْجُهَا وَ لَمْ يُدْخَلْ بِهَا وَ مَا لَهَا مِنَ الصَّدَاقِ وَ الْعِدَّةِ
chapter_title.en: Chapter 46 – The woman widowed from her husband and not having been slept with, and what is for her from the dower, and the waiting period

gradings: Allamah Baqir al-Majlisi: ضعيف - Mir‘at al ‘Uqul Fi Sharh Akhbar Al al Rasul (21/204)
Allamah Baqir al-Majlisi: ضعيف - Mir‘at al ‘Uqul Fi Sharh Akhbar Al al Rasul (21/204)
text: ا قُلْنَا لِأَبِي عَبْدِ اللَّهِ ( عليه السلام ) مَا تَقُولُ فِي رَجُلٍ تَزَوَّجَ امْرَأَةً ثُمَّ مَاتَ عَنْهَا وَ قَدْ فَرَضَ لَهَا الصَّدَاقَ فَقَالَ لَهَا نِصْفُ الصَّدَاقِ وَ تَرِثُهُ مِنْ كُلِّ شَيْ‏ءٍ وَ إِنْ مَاتَتْ فَهِيَ كَذَلِكَ.

en.hubeali: And by his chain, from Aban Bin Usman, from Ubeyd Bin Zurara, and Fazl Abu Al Abbas both said,

‘We said to Abu Abdullah<sup>asws</sup>, ‘What are you<sup>asws</sup> saying regarding a man who marries a woman, then dies from her, and the dower was obligated for her?’ So he<sup>asws</sup> said: ‘For her is half the dower, and she would inherit him from everything, and if she had died, so it would be like that’.
en.sarwar: 7. Through the same chain of narrators as that of the previous Hadith the following is narrated from Aban ibn ‘Uthman from ‘Ubayd ibn Zurarah and Fadl ibn al-‘Abbas who have said the following: “Once we asked abu ‘ Abd Allah, ‘Alayhi al-Salam, about the case of a man who marries a woman and then dies. He has set mahr (dower) for her. He (the Imam) said, ’She deserve one-half of the mahr (dower) and complete inheritance. If she dies the same rule applies in her favor.’”
